lead_profile_text: 2-bed terraced house, Bedminster, Bristol. Fully furnished (daughter lived there during university, recently vacated). On Rightmove — weighing sell vs STL in a poor sales market. Previous STL experience via local Bristol agency ended badly: damage discovered too late to claim. Minimal setup required: photography, key safe, smart thermostat. Second intelligence entry — previously appeared as entry 043.
emotional_profile: EX-STL with damage trauma at core. Not anti-STL — anti-bad-management. Pragmatic, factual, asks specific operational questions. Once reassured on damage notification and communication standards, conversion risk drops significantly. STL-during-sale framing is compelling given the weak property market.
green_light_trigger_phrases: weighing sell vs STL due to poor property market
STL during sale is a compelling position

top_objections.text: The previous agency didn't tell me until it was too late to claim — damage notification central trauma
top_objections.zac_response: AirCover protection + pre/post photography workflow; proactive damage notification as a core service standard differentiated from previous agency.

top_objections.text: Party damage risk — university area, concerned about student guest types in Bedminster
top_objections.zac_response: ID verification, £200 deposit, booking history screening; 30% direct bookings = lower-risk guest profile; AirCover up to £100k.

top_objections.text: Stayful is relatively new — credibility question
top_objections.zac_response: ~70 properties under management confirmed on call; team background mentioned; case studies to follow.

top_questions.text: How does guest vetting work in detail?
top_questions.answer: ID checks, booking history screening, £200 security deposit, £100k AirCover protection on Airbnb bookings.

top_questions.text: What is Stayful's track record and scale?
top_questions.answer: ~70 properties currently under management; team background confirmed; case studies to be shared.

what_moved_them: STL-during-sale framing — income while the property sits on the market in a poor sales climate
30% direct bookings as signal of lower-risk guest profile
what_fell_flat: Company age concern not fully resolved on the call
Damage notification differentiation needs reinforcing — management agreement/T&Cs sent after meeting

conversion_gaps.gap: Damage communication standard not clearly differentiated from previous agency on the call
conversion_gaps.how_to_fill: Management agreement + T&Cs sent within 15 minutes of call showing proactive notification workflow. Share case studies and portfolio references.

conversion_gaps.gap: No promotional offer made — warm status only
conversion_gaps.how_to_fill: Follow up before 30 June; consider promotional offer if Martyn shows hesitation after reviewing agreement.

offer_framing: No promotional discount applied. Standard 15%+VAT. Pitch led on STL-during-sale income opportunity and damage protection differentiation from previous agency.
zac_close_phrase: Zac to send management agreement + T&Cs within 15 minutes; Martyn to decide by 30 June
zac_income_intro: Year 2 net £2,080/month average. Worst month January £1,430; best month July £2,759. STL uplift +£10,678/year vs LTL — that's 75% more income, £890/month extra.
zac_transition_phrases: STL during the sale — you're generating income while the property sits on the market
30% direct bookings means lower-risk guest profile over time
zac_reassurance_phrases: £200 deposit + £100k AirCover — you're protected even if something does go wrong
Pre/post photography workflow means you know about any damage immediately — not after it's too late to claim
